## 0.4.0 (2026-05-15)

- Add retry-with-backoff to the OpenAI client
- Fix incorrect cost-per-token for Haiku 4.5
- Drop Python 3.11 support

## 0.3.0 (2026-04-22)

from __future__ import annotations

import httpx

BASE_URL = 'https://api.tensorzero.com'
API_KEY_HEADER = 'X-Api-Key'

# Default per-request timeout in seconds. Override per-call via
# the `timeout=` kwarg on send().
TIMEOUT_S = 30

class Client:
    def __init__(self, key: str):
        self._client = httpx.Client(timeout=TIMEOUT_S)
BASE_URL = 'api.tensorzero.com'
BASE_URL = 'api.tensorzero.com'
BASE_URL = 'api.tensorzero.com'

input.message: fix: point clients to prod URL

input.title: Hotfix: DB cursor race condition
input.body:
## Summary
Fixes the DB connection pool race condition reported in #87 where `cursor.execute()` calls were interleaving under concurrent load.

## Changes
- **src/db.py**: Added thread lock serialization around cursor creation and execution to prevent concurrent access to the database cursor.

## Details
The fix wraps the cursor creation and execution in a `with self._lock:` block to ensure only one thread can execute database operations at a time, eliminating the race condition.

Fixes #87
input.head: hotfix/db-cursor-race

input.title: fix: handle missing config gracefully
input.body: This fix adds a guard clause in the `load_config` function to raise a clean `FileNotFoundError` when the YAML configuration file is missing or empty. Previously, the function would fail with an unhelpful error when `read_yaml` returned `None`. Now it provides a clear, actionable error message that indicates the config file was not found or is empty, improving the debugging experience for users.
input.head: fix/null-config-handling

A few issues with edge cases — see inline. Otherwise looks great.

input.title: Hotfix: DB cursor race condition
input.body:
## Summary
This hotfix addresses the DB connection pool race condition reported in #87.

## Changes
- **src/db.py**: Added thread lock serialization around `cursor.execute()` call to prevent concurrent interleaving under load

## Issue
Fixes #87 - DB connection pool: cursor.execute() interleaves under concurrent load

## Details
The `execute()` method now wraps cursor operations with `self._lock` to ensure thread-safe access to the database cursor, preventing race conditions when multiple threads attempt concurrent database operations.
input.head: hotfix/db-cursor-race

test(themes): add dark mode test stub

input.title: Add dark mode theme
input.body:
## Summary
This PR adds a dark mode theme to the application.

## Changes
- **feat(themes): add dark mode stylesheet** — Introduces dark.css with CSS variables for dark mode colors (background, foreground, accent)
- **feat(themes): wire dark mode into theme loader** — Imports the new dark.css file into the theme index to make it available
- **test(themes): add dark mode test stub** — Adds initial test stub for dark mode functionality (to be wired up with theme loader)

## Details
The dark mode theme uses a data-theme attribute selector and defines three CSS variables:
- `--bg`: Dark background (#0d1117)
- `--fg`: Light foreground text (#e6edf3)
- `--accent`: Blue accent color (#58a6ff)
This breaks when `token` is None — add an early return.
Use TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS constant from config.py instead of hard-coding 3600.
Add a test for the expired-token branch.
Three issues — see inline. Otherwise the structure is good.
Heads up — the auth module also needs unit tests for the new code path. Happy to pair on it if useful.

This PR adds a dark mode theme to the application with the following changes:

- **feat(themes): add dark mode stylesheet** — Introduces `src/themes/dark.css` with dark mode color variables (dark background #0d1117, light foreground #e6edf3, and blue accent #58a6ff)

The dark mode can be activated by setting `data-theme='dark'` on the root element.

# Changelog

## 0.4.0 (2026-05-15)

- Add retry-with-backoff to the OpenAI client
- Fix incorrect cost-per-token for Haiku 4.5
- Drop Python 3.11 support

## 0.3.0 (2026-04-22)

input.title: fix: handle missing config gracefully
input.head: fix/null-config-handling
input.body: This fix adds a guard clause to the `load_config` function to properly handle cases where the YAML configuration file is missing or empty. When `read_yaml` returns `None`, the function now raises a clean `FileNotFoundError` with a descriptive message instead of allowing the code to fail with an unhelpful error when trying to access dictionary keys on a `None` value.
This needs to handle the case where `tokens` is empty — currently it raises IndexError.
A few issues with edge cases — see inline. Otherwise looks great.

input.body:
## Summary
This PR fixes the DB connection pool race condition reported in #87.

## Changes
- **src/db.py**: Added thread-safe locking around `cursor.execute()` calls to prevent concurrent interleaving of cursor operations under load.

## Details
The issue was that multiple threads could interleave cursor operations, causing data corruption. This fix wraps the cursor execution in a `threading.Lock` to serialize access.

Fixes #87

from __future__ import annotations

import httpx

BASE_URL = 'https://api.tensorzero.com'
API_KEY_HEADER = 'X-Api-Key'

# Default per-request timeout in seconds. Override per-call via
# the `timeout=` kwarg on send().
TIMEOUT_S = 30

class Client:

requires-python = ">=3.12"

# Changelog

## 0.4.0 (2026-05-15)

- Add retry-with-backoff to the OpenAI client
- Fix incorrect cost-per-token for Haiku 4.5
- Drop Python 3.11 support

## 0.3.0 (2026-04-22)

- Initial public release of the scratch repo
- Add caching layer for the Anthropic client

input.title: fix: handle missing config gracefully
input.body: This fix adds a guard clause in the `load_config` function to detect when the YAML configuration file is missing or empty. When `read_yaml` returns `None`, the function now raises a clean `FileNotFoundError` with a descriptive message, improving error handling and making it easier for users to diagnose configuration issues.
input.head: fix/null-config-handling

input.body: This needs to handle the case where `tokens` is empty — currently it raises IndexError.
A few issues with edge cases — see inline. Otherwise looks great.

from __future__ import annotations

import httpx

BASE_URL = 'https://api.tensorzero.com'
API_KEY_HEADER = 'X-Api-Key'

# Default per-request timeout in seconds. Override per-call via
# the `timeout=` kwarg on send().
TIMEOUT_S = 30

class Client:
    def __init__(self, key: str):
        self._client = httpx.Client(timeout=TIMEOUT_S)
BASE_URL = 'api.tensorzero.com'
BASE_URL = 'api.tensorzero.com'
BASE_URL = 'api.tensorzero.com'

input.title: Hotfix: DB cursor race condition
input.body:
## Summary
This PR fixes the DB connection pool race condition reported in #87.

## Changes
- **src/db.py**: Added thread-safe locking around `cursor.execute()` calls to prevent concurrent interleaving of SQL operations.

## Details
The issue was that multiple threads could interleave their cursor operations, causing data corruption and unpredictable behavior under concurrent load. This fix wraps the cursor execution in a lock to serialize access.

Fixes #87
input.head: hotfix/db-cursor-race
